question: Will we have a popular LLM fine-tuned on people's personal texts by June 1, 2026?
reason: No evidence of a popular LLM fine-tuned on personal texts with 20 million+ users by June 1, 2026.
evidence: Microsoft Research's 2026 study found that current LLM-based individual simulation methods do not pass the Individual Turing Test, indicating limitations in fine-tuning LLMs on personal data. ([microsoft.com](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/publication/individual-turing-test-a-case-study-of-llm-based-simulation-using-longitudinal-personal-data/?utm_source=openai))

OpenAI's 2026 fine-tuning guide emphasizes that fine-tuning is not always the best solution, suggesting that better prompt engineering or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) may be more effective in many cases. ([respan.ai](https://www.respan.ai/articles/openai-fine-tuning-guide?utm_source=openai))
As of May 2026, there is no publicly available information about an LLM fine-tuned on personal texts achieving 20 million+ users.
